title: FLAG-MODEL A2: audit_prefill returns flags for catalogue-survivors instead of raising; structural/FK/invariant stay hard-reject
description: audit_prefill (planner/_freetext_audit.py:1320-1338) currently raises ValueError on the first non-empty free-text survivors list, which the parsers propagate as a hard reject (apply exits non-zero, workflow halts). The three-valued model needs this single class - free-text proper-name survivors from the catalogue gate - to become a FLAG (admit-with-annotation) rather than a hard reject. audit_prefill should return a flags list (path, span, verdict from A1, reason) instead of raising. This is the load-bearing HARD/SOFT PARTITION: ONLY free-text catalogue-survivors become flags; every structural failure (unparseable JSON, missing/extra keys, wrong types, gates), coded-FK failure (improvement_type/planning_method/division/position/standard/frequency/timing/kind/enum), index/range check, and cross-field invariant (type-method, per-kind date rules, target-iff-kind, verbatim statement match) STAYS a hard reject (data unmergeable). FREETEXT_SELECTORS_BY_SPEC (:1301) already scopes which 12 of 14 specs/fields are free-text (flag-eligible); B2/F2 have none. Atomic gap; depends on A1.
impact: audit_prefill raises on the first free-text survivor, so a single catalogue-survivor hard-halts the whole apply/sequence instead of becoming a reviewable flag.
proposed_resolution: audit_prefill returns a flags list (path, span, verdict, reason) for free-text catalogue-survivors; structural/FK/invariant/index failures stay hard-reject. FREETEXT_SELECTORS_BY_SPEC scopes the 12 flag-eligible specs.
